title: Card Enhancements — Completeness Badge, Priority Badge, Counts, Spawn Chain, Decision Dates
problem: Feature cards show title, problem, tags, and actions but give no at-a-glance signal of how well-documented a feature is, what its priority is, how many decisions/limitations it has, or how it relates to its parent via spawnReason. Decision dates are stored but not displayed.

lineage.spawnReason: scope split: add completeness badge (0–100% from completenessOf), priority badge (P1–P5), decision/limitation count pills, clickable tags that activate filters, jump-to-graph button, decision dates in expanded view, spawn-chain strip in breadcrumb, stats bar above tree, deprecated card styling, and successCriteria moved to top of expanded content

analysis: Each feature card was showing only the essentials (title, problem, tags, actions) but leaving several rich fields invisible or poorly surfaced. Eight enhancements were bundled: (1) Completeness badge — completenessOf(f) scores 7 optional fields (analysis, implementation, decisions, successCriteria, knownLimitations, tags, domain) and shows a colour-coded percentage (green ≥80%, amber ≥50%, red <50%). (2) Priority badge — renders P1–P5 when the priority field is set. (3) Decision/limitation count pills — shows '3 decisions' and '2 limitations' as muted badges at a glance without expanding. (4) Clickable tags — each tag chip is now a <button> that calls onTagClick, toggling the corresponding tag filter in FilterBar; active tags are highlighted. (5) Jump-to-graph button — '◎ graph' in the card action row sets focusKey and switches view to 'graph'. (6) Decision dates — decision.date is rendered inline with each decision title in the expanded section. (7) Inline spawnReason on cards — every card with a lineage.spawnReason shows a '↳ reason' line directly below the problem text, surfacing the spawn context without requiring focus mode. (8) Spawn chain in breadcrumb — when the focused feature chain contains features with spawnReason, a strip below the breadcrumb shows each parent→child reason as a readable sentence. Additionally: stats bar above the tree shows total features, decisions, and domains as live counts; deprecated features get an 'is-deprecated' CSS class for distinct styling; successCriteria section moved from bottom to top of expanded content.
implementation: completenessOf(f: Feature): number exported from features.ts — checks 7 boolean conditions and returns Math.round(score * 100). FeatureCard renders a .completeness-badge with tier class (high/mid/low) computed inline via IIFE. Priority badge rendered as .priority-badge when node.priority != null. Count pills in .card-counts div rendered only when decisions or knownLimitations are non-empty. Tags converted from to

decisions.decision: completenessOf exported from features.ts and used in both FeatureCard and FeatureGraph
decisions.rationale: A single computation ensures card badge and graph node size always agree — no risk of divergence between the two surfaces.
alternativesConsidered: Inline computation per surface — risk of divergence
Store as computed field in feature.json — stale if schema changes

decisions.decision: Tags become filter-activating buttons, not decorative spans
decisions.rationale: Tags are the primary facet for navigating related features — making them clickable collapses the gap between 'I see a tag I want' and 'I filter by that tag' from multiple steps to one click.
alternativesConsidered: Keep as spans, filter only from FilterBar — friction
Open a modal on tag click — over-engineered for a filter action

decisions.decision: Spawn chain rendered as a strip below the breadcrumb, not inline in card body
decisions.rationale: The spawn chain describes the reasoning thread between features — placing it under the breadcrumb keeps it in the navigation layer, not the card content layer, which is the right semantic position.
alternativesConsidered: Inside the focused card body — buries it in content
Tooltip on breadcrumb crumbs — too small and hover-only

decisions.decision: successCriteria moved to top of expanded content
decisions.rationale: successCriteria is the 'definition of done' — it's the most important summary for a reader who expands a card. It should appear before implementation prose.
alternativesConsidered: Keep at bottom — last thing read, often missed
Show in card header always — too verbose for collapsed state

successCriteria: Every card shows a colour-coded completeness percentage and (when set) a priority badge in the header. Clicking any tag chip activates that filter in FilterBar and highlights the tag on the card. The expanded card shows successCriteria first, then analysis, then decisions with dates, then limitations. A '◎ graph' button jumps to graph view focused on that node. The breadcrumb strip shows spawnReason sentences for each ancestor step when they exist. The stats bar above the list shows live feature/decision/domain counts.
knownLimitations: Completeness score weights all 7 fields equally — a feature with one-line analysis scores the same as one with paragraphs
Jump-to-graph from a card doesn't animate the graph to the node immediately if the simulation is still settling
Spawn chain only shows steps that have a spawnReason — multi-hop chains with missing spawnReason silently skip those steps
Mobile action row can have up to 5 buttons (expand, show children, focus, json, graph) with no responsive collapse strategy

revisions.author: claude
revisions.reason: Initial field population — all enhancements implemented in App.tsx and features.ts; fields authored from code inspection

revisions.author: claude
revisions.reason: Fixed two inaccuracies: added missing inline spawnReason display on cards (p.spawn-reason, separate from breadcrumb spawn chain); corrected button count in knownLimitations from 3 to 5 (expand, show children, focus, json, graph). Analysis updated from 7 to 8 enhancements to include inline spawnReason.

statusHistory.reason: Two inaccuracies: inline spawnReason display on every card body (p.spawn-reason ↳ text) is not mentioned — only the breadcrumb spawn chain is documented; knownLimitations says 'three buttons' in action row but there are five (expand, show children, focus, json, graph).

annotations.author: lac advance
annotations.body: Two inaccuracies: inline spawnReason display on every card body (p.spawn-reason ↳ text) is not mentioned — only the breadcrumb spawn chain is documented; knownLimitations says 'three buttons' in action row but there are five (expand, show children, focus, json, graph).
